decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo by using cards like Stifle, Foil, and Counterspell to protect it. The deck aims to control the game with counterspells and card filtering spells like Opt and Portent while also having the flexibility to disrupt the opponent with cards like Thwart and Misdirection. The inclusion of Peek and Vision Charm allows for additional card advantage and information gathering, while the singleton Powder Keg provides additional removal options. The overall strategy is to efficiently interact with the opponent while setting up the game-winning combo.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing the Oath of Druids card to put powerful creatures like Terravore into play quickly. It also includes disruptive cards like Sphere of Resistance and Swords to Plowshares to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck focuses on mana denial strategies with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, while also including card advantage engines like Sylvan Library. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's plans, control the game tempo, and eventually overwhelm them with powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive Goblin creatures while utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to bring them onto the battlefield for free. It also includes cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's mana base and maintain board control. The deck also features cards like Goblin Matron and Goblin Ringleader to search for and draw more Goblins, ensuring a steady stream of threats. Overall, the key concept is to swarm the opponent with fast, efficient Goblin creatures and maintain pressure throughout the game.

The deck focuses on using Stifle to disrupt opponent's key plays while setting up the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo to quickly win the game. Counterspells like Foil and Misdirection are used to protect the combo pieces, with support from cards like Portent and Opt to find necessary pieces. The deck aims to control the game through counter magic and efficient card filtering, setting up a quick and decisive victory using the Stiflenought combo.

The main concept of this BW Control deck is to control the game through a combination of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, along with hand disruption through Duress and Cabal Therapy. The deck also utilizes board wipes like Wrath of God and Decree of Pain to clear threats. Additionally, the deck has card advantage engines in Skeletal Scrying and Eternal Dragon, as well as the ability to generate tokens with Decree of Justice. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of removal, disruption, and card advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming your opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to quickly get creatures onto the battlefield, combined with powerful lords like Goblin King and Goblin Warchief to boost their stats, the deck aims to deal fast and consistent damage to the opponent. Wasteland and Rishadan Port can disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Siege-Gang Commander provides additional reach with its token generation ability. Overall, this deck utilizes the speed and synergy of Goblins to apply pressure and win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures, using cards like Goblin Warchief to reduce mana costs and increase speed, and Goblin Piledriver to deal massive damage. It includes utility cards like Gempalm Incinerator for removal and Goblin Matron for tutoring. The deck also uses land disruption with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's mana base. With a mix of efficient creatures and disruption, the deck aims to close out games quickly with a relentless onslaught of Goblins.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells while maintaining control of the board. By utilizing efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade, alongside disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while consistently applying pressure. The inclusion of cards like Dark Ritual allows for explosive starts, while cards like Wasteland and Cursed Scroll help control the opponent's resources. Ultimately, the deck aims to quickly close out the game through a combination of creature damage and discard effects.

The main concept of this goblin aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of aggressive creatures. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to search for key goblin creatures like Goblin Warchief and Siege-Gang Commander, the deck aims to establish a strong board presence early in the game. Cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter provide removal and additional reach to finish off the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Rishadan Port and Smother to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain tempo.

This deck focuses on assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to quickly bring out a powerful creature while controlling the game with counterspells and card draw. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's plans, counter key spells, and protect the combo pieces until you can overwhelm your opponent with a massive threat. By carefully managing resources and timing, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure a quick victory through the efficient execution of a powerful combo.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent using Stasis to prevent them from untapping their permanents, while using countermagic and card draw spells to maintain control of the game. The combination of Forsaken City and Gush allows for additional mana advantage, while cards like Thwart and Chain of Vapor provide versatile answers to opposing threats. The deck can also win through alternative win conditions like Treva's Ruins or Brain Freeze, making it a flexible and resilient combo deck in the Premodern format.

The main strategy revolves around using control elements like Mana Leak, Swords to Plowshares, and Counterspell to disrupt the opponent's plans while generating card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Standstill. Landstill also relies on utilizing land destruction cards like Dust Bowl to further disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to maintain board control through a combination of removal spells and counter magic, eventually overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats like Mishra's Factory and Decree of Justice.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle, denying the opponent from countering or disrupting the combo. The deck aims to control the game with a suite of counterspells and card draw spells while setting up the combo pieces. By efficiently using cards like Gush and Foil, this deck aims to stifle the opponent's game plan while capitalizing on the speed and power of the Dreadnought combo.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful enchantment-based combo using cards like Argothian Enchantress, Opalescence, and Mirri's Guile. By ramping up mana with Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, the player can quickly set up a strong board presence. Sterling Grove and Seal of Cleansing provide protection and removal, while cards like Parallax Wave and Elephant Grass control the battlefield. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of card draw, mana acceleration, and enchantment synergy, ultimately leading to victory through a well-timed combo.

The focus of this deck is to control and manipulate the game through a combination of countering opponent's spells, disrupting their strategy with cards like Meddling Mage and Armageddon, and drawing cards for card advantage. Psychatog is used as a win condition, becoming a powerful threat with the accumulation of cards in the graveyard. The deck also utilizes efficient mana sources like Mox Diamond and card draw spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to maintain its control over the game.

This deck focuses on quickly getting a Phyrexian Dreadnought onto the battlefield and using Stifle effects to counter any potential drawbacks. The strategy relies on disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and card draw while setting up the combo. The deck also includes various utility cards like Portent and Vision Charm to control the game until the combo can be assembled. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to efficiently and effectively combine the Dreadnought with Stifle effects to create a powerful win condition.
